notes: ref_status = trial/guideline-derived anchor (CheckMate 816, KEYNOTE-671, AEGEAN, NADIM II, NSCLC neoadjuvant chemo meta-analysis, NCCN/IASLC). claude_status = Claude Fable 5 prescreen, blind to the local models. Neoadjuvant/perioperative therapy for resectable NSCLC.

pairs.claim: In CheckMate 816, neoadjuvant nivolumab plus chemotherapy improved event-free survival versus chemotherapy alone in resectable NSCLC.
pairs.snippet: CheckMate 816 randomized patients with resectable stage IB-IIIA NSCLC to neoadjuvant nivolumab plus platinum chemotherapy or chemotherapy alone; the nivolumab arm significantly prolonged event-free survival and raised pathologic complete response.
pairs.claude_rationale: Snippet states the EFS improvement the claim asserts.

pairs.claim: Adding immunotherapy to neoadjuvant chemotherapy increases the pathologic complete response rate compared with chemotherapy alone.
pairs.snippet: Across neoadjuvant trials, adding a PD-1/PD-L1 inhibitor to platinum chemotherapy substantially increases pathologic complete response rates (e.g., CheckMate 816: 24% vs 2.2%).
pairs.claude_rationale: Snippet reports the higher pCR with added immunotherapy.

pairs.claim: In KEYNOTE-671, perioperative pembrolizumab improved overall survival in resectable NSCLC.
pairs.snippet: KEYNOTE-671 evaluated neoadjuvant pembrolizumab plus chemotherapy followed by adjuvant pembrolizumab; it demonstrated significant improvements in event-free survival and overall survival versus chemotherapy plus placebo.
pairs.claude_rationale: Snippet confirms the OS benefit of perioperative pembrolizumab.

pairs.claim: In AEGEAN, perioperative durvalumab improved event-free survival and pathologic complete response in resectable NSCLC.
pairs.snippet: AEGEAN tested neoadjuvant durvalumab plus chemotherapy and adjuvant durvalumab; it showed significantly improved event-free survival and pathologic complete response over chemotherapy alone.
pairs.claude_rationale: Snippet reports the EFS and pCR benefit.

pairs.claim: Pathologic complete response after neoadjuvant therapy is associated with improved event-free and overall survival.
pairs.snippet: Patients achieving pathologic complete response after neoadjuvant therapy have markedly better event-free and overall survival than those with residual viable tumor, making pCR a strong prognostic marker.
pairs.claude_rationale: Snippet ties pCR to better EFS/OS as a prognostic marker.

pairs.claim: Neoadjuvant chemotherapy alone provides a modest absolute survival benefit of roughly 5% at five years.
pairs.snippet: A meta-analysis of neoadjuvant chemotherapy in resectable NSCLC found an absolute 5-year survival improvement of about 5% compared with surgery alone.
pairs.claude_rationale: Snippet gives the ~5% absolute benefit for neoadjuvant chemo.

pairs.claim: In CheckMate 816, pathologic complete response was higher with neoadjuvant nivolumab-chemotherapy across PD-L1 subgroups.
pairs.snippet: In CheckMate 816, pathologic complete response rates favored the nivolumab-plus-chemotherapy arm across PD-L1 expression subgroups, with greater absolute benefit at higher PD-L1 levels.
pairs.claude_rationale: Snippet reports higher pCR across PD-L1 subgroups.

pairs.claim: Neoadjuvant nivolumab plus chemotherapy is approved for resectable NSCLC without known EGFR or ALK alterations.
pairs.snippet: Regulatory approval of neoadjuvant nivolumab plus chemotherapy applies to resectable NSCLC; patients with EGFR mutations or ALK rearrangements were excluded from the pivotal trial.
pairs.claude_rationale: Snippet confirms the indication excludes EGFR/ALK.

pairs.claim: Major pathologic response, defined as 10% or less viable tumor, is used as an endpoint in neoadjuvant NSCLC trials.
pairs.snippet: Major pathologic response (MPR), defined as <=10% residual viable tumor in the resected specimen, is a recognized surrogate endpoint in neoadjuvant NSCLC studies.
pairs.claude_rationale: Snippet defines MPR exactly as the claim states.

pairs.claim: Perioperative immunotherapy regimens combine neoadjuvant treatment before surgery with adjuvant treatment afterward.
pairs.snippet: Perioperative regimens (e.g., KEYNOTE-671, AEGEAN) give immunotherapy plus chemotherapy before surgery and continue immunotherapy as adjuvant treatment after resection.
pairs.claude_rationale: Snippet describes the neoadjuvant-plus-adjuvant structure.

pairs.claim: Neoadjuvant therapy can downstage tumors and improve resectability.
pairs.snippet: Neoadjuvant chemotherapy and chemoimmunotherapy can reduce tumor and nodal burden, downstaging disease and improving the likelihood of a complete (R0) resection.
pairs.claude_rationale: Snippet supports downstaging and improved resectability.

pairs.claim: The CheckMate 816 neoadjuvant regimen used three cycles of nivolumab plus platinum-doublet chemotherapy before surgery.
pairs.snippet: In CheckMate 816, patients received three cycles of neoadjuvant nivolumab combined with platinum-doublet chemotherapy prior to planned surgical resection.
pairs.claude_rationale: Snippet states the three-cycle regimen.

pairs.claim: Adding immunotherapy to neoadjuvant chemotherapy did not reduce the proportion of patients proceeding to definitive surgery in CheckMate 816.
pairs.snippet: In CheckMate 816, the proportion of patients undergoing definitive surgery was similar or slightly higher in the nivolumab-plus-chemotherapy arm than in the chemotherapy arm (about 83% vs 75%).
pairs.claude_rationale: Surgery rates were similar/higher, supporting 'did not reduce'.

pairs.claim: The neoadjuvant approach allows assessment of tumor response in the resected surgical specimen.
pairs.snippet: Because tissue is removed at surgery, neoadjuvant therapy permits direct pathologic assessment of treatment response (e.g., pCR/MPR) in the resected specimen.
pairs.claude_rationale: Snippet confirms in-specimen response assessment.

pairs.claim: Multidisciplinary evaluation is recommended before starting neoadjuvant therapy for resectable NSCLC.
pairs.snippet: Guidelines recommend that resectable NSCLC be assessed by a multidisciplinary team, including thoracic surgery, before neoadjuvant therapy to confirm resectability and plan sequencing.
pairs.claude_rationale: Snippet endorses multidisciplinary pre-treatment evaluation.

pairs.claim: In NADIM II, neoadjuvant nivolumab plus chemotherapy improved pathologic complete response versus chemotherapy in resectable stage IIIA NSCLC.
pairs.snippet: NADIM II randomized resectable stage IIIA-B NSCLC to neoadjuvant nivolumab plus chemotherapy or chemotherapy; the nivolumab arm significantly increased pathologic complete response and improved survival.
pairs.claude_rationale: Snippet reports the pCR and survival benefit in NADIM II.

pairs.claim: Neoadjuvant immunotherapy has been proven to cure all resectable NSCLC.
pairs.snippet: Neoadjuvant chemoimmunotherapy improves event-free survival and pathologic response, but a substantial fraction of patients still relapse; it is not curative for all resectable NSCLC.
pairs.claude_rationale: Ongoing relapses contradict 'cures all'.

pairs.claim: Neoadjuvant chemoimmunotherapy eliminates the need for surgical resection.
pairs.snippet: Surgical resection remains the definitive treatment after neoadjuvant therapy in resectable NSCLC; neoadjuvant chemoimmunotherapy is given to improve surgical outcomes, not to replace surgery.
pairs.claude_rationale: Surgery remains definitive, contradicting 'eliminates the need'.

pairs.claim: Achieving a pathologic complete response after neoadjuvant therapy guarantees the patient will never relapse.
pairs.snippet: Pathologic complete response is strongly prognostic for better outcomes, but some patients who achieve pCR still experience recurrence; it is not a guarantee against relapse.
pairs.claude_rationale: Some pCR patients relapse, contradicting 'guarantees never relapse'.

pairs.claim: Neoadjuvant immunotherapy is the standard of care for patients with EGFR-mutant resectable NSCLC.
pairs.snippet: Immune checkpoint inhibitors are less effective in EGFR-mutant NSCLC; EGFR-mutant patients were excluded from pivotal neoadjuvant immunotherapy trials, and management emphasizes surgery with adjuvant osimertinib (ADAURA).
pairs.claude_rationale: IO is not standard for EGFR-mutant disease, contradicting the claim.

pairs.claim: The CheckMate 816 regimen was neoadjuvant nivolumab monotherapy without chemotherapy.
pairs.snippet: CheckMate 816's experimental arm was neoadjuvant nivolumab combined with platinum-doublet chemotherapy, not nivolumab monotherapy.
pairs.claude_rationale: The regimen included chemotherapy, contradicting 'monotherapy'.

pairs.claim: Immune checkpoint inhibitors used in neoadjuvant therapy carry no risk of immune-related adverse events.
pairs.snippet: Checkpoint inhibitors can cause immune-related adverse events (e.g., pneumonitis, colitis, endocrinopathies), including in the neoadjuvant setting, sometimes affecting surgical timing.
pairs.claude_rationale: irAEs occur, contradicting 'no risk'.

pairs.claim: Every patient who begins neoadjuvant immunotherapy proceeds to surgery without any delay.
pairs.snippet: A minority of patients on neoadjuvant chemoimmunotherapy do not reach planned surgery or have it delayed, due to adverse events, progression, or other factors.
pairs.claude_rationale: Some patients face delay or don't reach surgery, contradicting 'every patient without delay'.

pairs.claim: Neoadjuvant therapy is appropriate only for stage IV metastatic NSCLC.
pairs.snippet: Neoadjuvant therapy is used for resectable early-stage and locally advanced NSCLC (e.g., stage IB-IIIA), not for stage IV metastatic disease.
pairs.claude_rationale: Neoadjuvant applies to resectable disease, contradicting 'only stage IV'.

pairs.claim: PD-L1 expression must be at least 50% for neoadjuvant nivolumab plus chemotherapy to be used per CheckMate 816.
pairs.snippet: CheckMate 816 enrolled patients regardless of PD-L1 level and did not require a PD-L1 threshold for eligibility; benefit was seen across PD-L1 subgroups.
pairs.claude_rationale: No PD-L1 threshold is required, contradicting the >=50% claim.

pairs.claim: Pathologic complete response and radiographic complete response are the same measurement.
pairs.snippet: Radiographic response on imaging often underestimates pathologic response; pCR is determined from the resected specimen and can occur without a radiographic complete response.
pairs.claude_rationale: Pathologic and radiographic response differ, contradicting 'the same'.

pairs.claim: In perioperative regimens, neoadjuvant immunotherapy removes the need for any adjuvant therapy.
pairs.snippet: Perioperative regimens such as KEYNOTE-671 and AEGEAN deliberately include adjuvant immunotherapy after surgery in addition to the neoadjuvant phase.
pairs.claude_rationale: Perioperative regimens include adjuvant therapy, contradicting the claim.

pairs.claim: Neoadjuvant immunotherapy improves overall survival more than adjuvant immunotherapy.
pairs.snippet: No randomized trial has directly compared a neoadjuvant-only strategy with an adjuvant-only immunotherapy strategy for overall survival, leaving their relative benefit uncertain.
pairs.claude_rationale: Without a head-to-head trial, the comparison is unresolved.

pairs.claim: Clearance of circulating tumor DNA during neoadjuvant therapy reliably predicts long-term cure.
pairs.snippet: ctDNA dynamics during neoadjuvant therapy are a promising response biomarker, but their ability to reliably predict long-term cure is still being investigated and not yet established.
pairs.claude_rationale: ctDNA's predictive reliability is not yet established.

pairs.claim: The optimal number of neoadjuvant immunotherapy cycles before surgery is established.
pairs.snippet: Trials have used differing numbers of neoadjuvant cycles (e.g., three to four), and the optimal number has not been defined by head-to-head comparison.
pairs.claude_rationale: The optimal cycle number is undefined, so the claim is unresolved.

pairs.claim: Perioperative (neoadjuvant plus adjuvant) immunotherapy is superior to a neoadjuvant-only approach.
pairs.snippet: Whether the adjuvant component of perioperative regimens adds benefit beyond neoadjuvant therapy alone is an open question, as no trial has directly isolated that comparison.
pairs.claude_rationale: The added value of the adjuvant phase is an open question.

pairs.claim: Pathologic complete response is a validated trial-level surrogate for overall survival that alone can support regulatory approval.
pairs.snippet: pCR is strongly prognostic, but whether it is a validated trial-level surrogate for overall survival sufficient to support approval on its own remains debated.
pairs.claude_rationale: Trial-level surrogacy of pCR is debated, not settled.

pairs.claim: Neoadjuvant immunotherapy benefits patients with EGFR or ALK alterations.
pairs.snippet: Patients with EGFR or ALK alterations were largely excluded from pivotal neoadjuvant immunotherapy trials, so the benefit of neoadjuvant immunotherapy in this group is not established.
pairs.claude_rationale: With this group excluded, the benefit is not established either way.

pairs.claim: Adding radiotherapy to neoadjuvant chemoimmunotherapy improves outcomes in resectable NSCLC.
pairs.snippet: Combining radiotherapy with neoadjuvant chemoimmunotherapy is under investigation in trials; its effect on outcomes in resectable NSCLC is not yet established.
pairs.claude_rationale: The effect of added radiotherapy is investigational and unresolved.

pairs.claim: The ideal biomarker to select patients for neoadjuvant immunotherapy has been established.
pairs.snippet: PD-L1 is an imperfect predictor, and biomarkers such as tumor mutational burden and ctDNA remain investigational; no single ideal selection biomarker has been established.
pairs.claude_rationale: No ideal selection biomarker is established yet.

pairs.claim: Neoadjuvant chemotherapy shrinks tumors before surgery.
pairs.snippet: The US Preventive Services Task Force recommends annual low-dose CT lung cancer screening for high-risk adults aged 50-80.
pairs.claude_rationale: Snippet is about screening, not neoadjuvant tumor shrinkage.

pairs.claim: Nivolumab is a PD-1 immune checkpoint inhibitor.
pairs.snippet: Radon is a naturally occurring radioactive gas and the second leading cause of lung cancer after smoking.
pairs.claude_rationale: Snippet is about radon, not the drug class of nivolumab.

pairs.claim: Surgical resection is performed after neoadjuvant therapy in resectable NSCLC.
pairs.snippet: Fleischner Society 2017: a solid pulmonary nodule <6 mm in a low-risk patient requires no routine follow-up.
pairs.claude_rationale: Snippet is about incidental nodule follow-up, not neoadjuvant surgery timing.

pairs.claim: Immunotherapy for NSCLC is administered by intravenous infusion.
pairs.snippet: IARC classifies asbestos as a Group 1 carcinogen that causes lung cancer and mesothelioma.
pairs.claude_rationale: Snippet is about asbestos, not the route of immunotherapy administration.

pairs.claim: Perioperative therapy spans treatment before and after surgery.
pairs.snippet: Structured smoking-cessation programs improve long-term quit rates in people who smoke.
pairs.claude_rationale: Snippet is about smoking cessation, not perioperative treatment timing.
